children.name: Prionailurus
children.description: Prionailurus — a genus of cats in the family Felidae.

children.name: Prionailurus bengalensis
children.description: The leopard cat is a small wild cat with dark spots on the tawny-coloured body and limbs. It has two dark stripes running from the eyes to the ears and smaller white streaks running from the eyes to the nose, and two to four rows of elongated spots along its back. Its tail is about half the size of its head-to-body length.

children.name: Prionailurus iriomotensis
children.description: The Iriomote cat is a subspecies of the leopard cat that lives exclusively on the Japanese island of Iriomote.

children.name: Prionailurus planiceps
children.description: The flat-headed cat is a small wild cat with short reddish-brown fur. Its head is elongated, and its ears are rounded. Its slender body is 41 to 50 cm long with a tail of 13 to 15 cm, and it weighs 1.5 to 2.5 kg.

children.name: Prionailurus rubiginosus
children.description: The rusty-spotted cat is one of the cat family's smallest members. It is native to India, Nepal and Sri Lanka. Since 2016, it has been listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List as the global population is affected by loss and destruction of its prime habitat, deciduous forests.

children.name: Prionailurus viverrinus
children.description: The fishing cat is a medium-sized wild cat of South and Southeast Asia. It has a deep yellowish-grey fur with black lines and spots. Adults have a head-to-body length of 57 to 78 cm, with a 20 to 30 cm long tail.

children.name: Leopardus
children.description: Leopardus — a genus of cats in the family Felidae.

children.name: Felis vorohuensis
children.description: Felis vorohuensis — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus braccatus
children.description: The Pantanal cat is a Pampas cat subspecies, a small wild cat native to South America. It is named after the Pantanal wetlands in central South America, where it inhabits mainly grassland, shrubland, savannas and deciduous forests.

children.name: Leopardus colocolo
children.description: The Pampas cat is a small wild cat native to South America. It is listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List as habitat conversion and destruction may cause the population to decline in the future.

children.name: Leopardus ellioti
children.description: Leopardus ellioti — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus guigna
children.description: The kodkod, also called güiña, is the smallest felid species native to the Americas. It lives primarily in central and southern Chile, as well as marginally in adjoining areas of Argentina. Between 1996 and 2002, it was listed as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List as the total population was thought to be fewer than 10,000 mature individuals; it is threatened by persecution, habitat loss and prey base.

children.name: Leopardus guttulus
children.description: Leopardus guttulus, the southern tigrina or southern tiger cat, is a small wild cat species native to Brazil, Argentina and Paraguay.

children.name: Leopardus hernandesii
children.description: Leopardus hernandesii — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus japonensis
children.description: Leopardus japonensis — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus pajeros
children.description: The Pampas cat is a small wild cat native to South America. It is listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List as habitat conversion and destruction may cause the population to decline in the future.

children.name: Leopardus perniger
children.description: Leopardus perniger — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus pictus
children.description: Leopardus pictus — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus poliopardus
children.description: Leopardus poliopardus — a species in the genus Leopardus, family Felidae. Also known as cats.

children.name: Leopardus tigrinus
children.description: The oncilla, also known as the northern tiger cat, little spotted cat, and tigrillo, is a small spotted cat ranging from Central America to central Brazil. It is listed as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List, and the population is threatened by deforestation and conversion of habitat to agricultural land.

children.name: Leopardus wiedii
children.description: Category:Taxa named by Heinrich Rudolf Schinz.

children.name: Felis catus
children.description: The cat, also called domestic cat and house cat, is a small domesticated carnivorous mammal. It is an obligate carnivore, requiring a predominantly meat-based diet. Its retractable claws are adapted to killing small prey species such as mice and rats.

children.name: Lynx lynx
children.description: The Eurasian lynx is one of the four extant species within the medium-sized wild cat genus Lynx. It is widely distributed from Northern, Central and Eastern Europe to Central Asia and Siberia, the Tibetan Plateau and the Himalayas. It inhabits temperate and boreal forests up to an elevation of 5,500 m (18,000 ft).
